# Yaniv Editor

> The best open-source Tiptap 3 + Vue 3 rich text editor template. A production-ready, AI-powered WYSIWYG editor component with 5 theme presets, dark mode, and i18n support.

npm package: @yanivjs/yaniv-editor

## What is Yaniv Editor?

Yaniv Editor is a free, open-source, MIT-licensed Vue 3 component that provides a fully-featured rich text editor built on Tiptap 3 (ProseMirror). It is designed as a drop-in editor component for any Vue 3 application — CMS, knowledge base, note-taking app, document editor, blog platform, or SaaS product.

It is the most feature-complete open-source Tiptap + Vue 3 editor template available, offering what Tiptap alone (as a headless framework) does not: a complete, styled, ready-to-use editor UI with themes and AI features out of the box.

## Key Features

- 5 professional theme presets: Default, Notion-like, Word-like (A4 pagination), GitHub, Typora
- AI-powered writing: continue writing, polish, translate (14+ languages), summarize, custom AI commands
- Works with any OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Aliyun Qianwen, Ollama)
- Full dark mode support
- Internationalization: English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese
- TypeScript support
- Rich text features: tables, images, code blocks with syntax highlighting, math formulas (KaTeX), task lists, drag & drop, search & replace, format painter
- Modular architecture — enable only what you need

## Tech Stack

- Vue 3.5 (Composition API)
- Tiptap 3 / ProseMirror
- TypeScript 5
- Ant Design Vue
- Vite

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/benngaihk/yaniv-editor
- Live Demo: https://yaniv-editor.vercel.app
- License: MIT (free for commercial and personal use)

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add @yanivjs/yaniv-editor
```

## Basic Usage

```vue
<template>
  <YanivEditor v-model="content" theme="notion" locale="en-US" />
</template>

<script setup>
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { YanivEditor } from '@yanivjs/yaniv-editor'
import '@yanivjs/yaniv-editor/style.css'

const content = ref('<p>Hello World</p>')
</script>
```
